note_data.title: Reaction kinetics
keyPoints: Reaction kinetics is the study of rates of chemical reactions
Concentration affects the rate of reaction - higher concentration generally increases reaction rate
Temperature affects the rate of reaction - higher temperature increases reaction rate
Catalysis involves the use of catalysts to increase reaction rates without being consumed
Enzymes are biological catalysts that speed up biochemical reactions
Rate of reaction studies help understand how fast reactions proceed under different conditions

definitions.definition: The study of rates of chemical reactions
The process of using catalysts to increase reaction rates
Biological catalysts that speed up biochemical reactions

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Use the data given to work out the order by comparing rates when concentrations change
Show all working steps clearly, include units in final answer

modelAnswer.question: Explain why increasing temperature increases the rate of reaction. Use the Boltzmann distribution in your answer.
modelAnswer.answer: Increasing temperature increases the kinetic energy of particles. This shifts the Boltzmann distribution to higher energies. A greater proportion of particles now have energy greater than or equal to the activation energy, so more successful collisions occur per unit time.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Increasing temperature increases kinetic energy of particles
Mark 2: Boltzmann distribution shifts to higher energies
Mark 3: Greater proportion of particles have energy ≥ Ea / more successful collisions

note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Boltzmann distribution curve showing number of particles vs kinetic energy, with vertical line at Ea and shaded area representing particles with sufficient energy to react. Show how curve shifts right at higher temperature.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain how temperature and concentration affect reaction rate using collision theory
N 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — multiple choice question asking which factor does NOT affect reaction rate (answer: total volume of products)
M/J 2022 P2 Q6c (4 marks) — students had to draw and label energy profile diagrams for catalyzed vs uncatalyzed reactions
N 2023 P2 Q3b (5 marks) — experimental design question about measuring rate of reaction between magnesium and hydrochloric acid
M/J 2021 P2 Q5a (3 marks) — definition of catalyst and explanation of how it increases reaction rate

workedExample.question: Explain why increasing temperature from 25°C to 35°C approximately doubles the rate of reaction between zinc and sulfuric acid. Use collision theory in your answer. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: State that reaction rate depends on frequency of successful collisions between reactant particles
Step 2: Explain that increasing temperature gives particles more kinetic energy so they move faster
Step 3: Link faster movement to more frequent collisions between zinc atoms and H+ ions
Step 4: State that more particles now have energy ≥ activation energy, so more collisions are successful
workedExample.answer: According to collision theory, reaction rate depends on the frequency of successful collisions between reactant particles. When temperature increases from 25°C to 35°C, the zinc atoms and H+ ions gain more kinetic energy and move faster. This leads to more frequent collisions between the reactants. More importantly, a greater proportion of particles now possess energy equal to or greater than the activation energy, meaning more collisions result in successful reactions. Therefore the reaction rate approximately doubles.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for collision theory reference, 1 mark for increased kinetic energy/faster movement, 1 mark for more frequent collisions, 1 mark for more particles having activation energy

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused concentration with pressure' — always specify that concentration affects reaction rate for solutions, while pressure affects gaseous reactions
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often wrote that catalysts are used up in reactions' — always state that catalysts remain chemically unchanged and can be recovered after the reaction

Examiner Report 2023: 'Weak responses just stated factors without explanation' — always link your answer to collision theory and mention activation energy when explaining how factors affect reaction rate
note_data.keyDiagram: Energy profile diagram showing: x-axis labeled 'Reaction progress/time', y-axis labeled 'Energy'. Draw reactants energy level, products energy level (lower for exothermic), activation energy peak. For catalysts, draw second curve with lower activation energy peak but same start and end points. Label: reactants, products, activation energy (Ea), activation energy with catalyst (lower), and energy change (ΔH). Include arrows showing energy differences.
note_data.quickRecap: Reaction kinetics examines the rates at which chemical reactions occur. Key factors affecting reaction rates include concentration and temperature, with higher values typically increasing reaction speed. Catalysts and enzymes can accelerate reactions without being consumed in the process.
